items.hook: The Automation is an indie, mythpunk novel by an anonymous author using the dual pen names B.L.A.
items.body: Gabbler, about the god Vulcan's Automata which function off their human Master's souls. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Aïcha Aminata Laïla Fofana was a Malian translator and author.
items.body: With Marriage: on copie (1994), she became the first woman in Mali to publish a novel. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Why praying for rain appears to work – but only in some places
items.body: In some parts of the world, the probability of rain rises with every day it doesn’t rain, and communities in these places are more likely to carry out rain-making rituals

items.hook: King Otto II and his Frankish army are defeated by the Muslim army of al-Qasim at Cape Colonna, Southern Italy.
items.body: Otto II, called the Red, was Holy Roman Emperor from 973 until his death in 983. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Stolthet och fördom is a three-act opéra comique by Daniel Nelson, to a libretto by Swedish playwright Sofia Fredén based on the popular 1813 English novel by Jane Austen.
items.body: The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Synthetic Sincerity review – Marc Isaacs’ AI interrogation grapples with identity and existence
items.body:
A combination of fact and fiction leaves the celebrated documentarian’s puzzling project about software training wanting for depth

Marc Isaacs’ new film is a curious, intriguing, semi-sincere affair that I couldn’t make friends wit

items.hook: Jane Campion remembers Sam Neill: ‘He was radiating peace, beaming love’
items.body:
The Piano director shares her memories of the actor on set – and the last time she saw him in hospital

  • Sam Neil

items.hook: Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 1-11 ditches off Polillo Island in the Philippines, killing one person and injuring 44.
items.body: Northwest Orient Airlines Flight 1–11 was a Northwest Orient Airlines flight from New York City to Manila, with stopovers in Seattle, Anchorage, Tokyo, and Okinawa. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Time is a 2007 Indian Malayalam-language psychological thriller film directed by Shaji Kailas and written by Rajesh Jayaraman.
items.body: The film features Suresh Gopi in a dual role as a father and son, along with Siddique, Vimala Raman and Padmapriya in important roles. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: As a Reincarnated Aristocrat, I'll Use My Appraisal Skill to Rise in the World is a Japanese light novel series written by Miraijin A.
items.body: The series originated on the Shōsetsuka ni Narō website, before being published in print with illustrations by Jimmy by Kodansha beginning in July 2020. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A man ploughs a truck into a Bastille Day celebration in Nice, France, killing 86 people and injuring another 434 before being shot by police.
items.body: On the evening of 14 July 2016, a 19-tonne cargo truck was deliberately driven into crowds of people celebrating Bastille Day on the Promenade des Anglais in Nice, France, resulting in the deaths of 86 people and injuring 458 others. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Bectu head Philippa Childs to leave UK union
items.body: She will stand down in January 2027.

items.hook: Opening of the first major US world's fair, the Exhibition of the Industry of All Nations in New York City.
items.body: A world's fair, also known as a universal exhibition, is a large global exhibition designed to showcase the achievements of nations. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Disney’s ‘Moana’ tops UK-Ireland box office with £2.8m as ‘Toy Story 5’ passes £40m
items.body: Meanwhile, Studiocanal’s ’Evil Dead Burn’ opened in fourth place.

items.hook: Sam Neill’s final films tell us a lot about who he was as an actor – and why we’ll miss him | Luke Buckmaster
items.body: A character actor in a leading man’s body, he gave balls-to-the-wall performances in a subversively varied career

items.hook: Blue singer Lee Ryan loses plane assault appeal
items.body: The Blue singer was challenging a conviction for assaulting a cabin crew member on a flight.

items.hook: Storming of the Bastille in Paris. This event escalates the widespread discontent into the French Revolution. Bastille Day is still celebrated annually in France.
items.body: The Storming of the Bastille, also known as Fall of the Bastille, which occurred in Paris, France, on 14 July 1789, was an act of political violence by revolutionary insurgents who attempted to storm and seize control of the medieval armoury, fortress, and political prison known as the Bastille. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Finnish War: the Battle of Lapua is fought.
items.body: The Finnish War was fought between the Kingdom of Sweden and the Russian Empire from 21 February 1808 to 17 September 1809 as part of the Napoleonic Wars. One of those things you can't un-know.
